note: Phase 1+2 skipped for this promoted iter (the shadow already ran a full engine). Straight to Step 2.5 -> Step 3, per Decide outcome 2.

phase3_findings.description: Prose-head fixture's completeness asserted by a comment, not a check; decoupled from SKILL.md.
`gh api graphql` omitted from the fixture -> the diff-added 'FULL population' comment is false against HEAD (documented_falsehood -> self-contradicting-diff carve-out, non-demotable).
_jobs_err temp file leaked on every in-loop unavailable path.
devflow.yml's inline PR_NUM/HEAD_SHA resolution has no behavioural test; correctness rests on an unenforced coupling to resolve-command-trigger.sh's output shape.
SECURITY header omitted the conclusion-field 40-char cap.

tools_allowlist_line() returned the first of several TOOLS='...' matches and raised only on zero, so a second allowlist would be silently ignored -- a fail-open in the matcher every #363 pin depends on. Iteration 1 pushed this back as claim-quality; the widens-surface guard disqualifies that skip because extract-command-heads.py is new code in this PR, so 'pre-existing / out of scope' cannot apply. Fixed rather than deferred.

fix_decisions.claim_text: devflow.yml's PR_NUM/HEAD_SHA resolution is untested and rests on an unenforced coupling
fix_decisions.evidence: Over-grade shape 3 FLAGGED: uncorroborated single-source from pr-test-analyzer, an empirical over-grader, with no other Phase-3 agent raising it and no Phase-2 FAIL covering it. Evaluated on the observable fail-direction, not the merits: today `resolve-command-trigger.sh` emits exactly `/devflow:<cmd> <n>` (independently verified twice this run, by VC-13 and VC-16), so `grep -oE '[0-9]+' | head -1` extracts the correct PR number and the observable blast radius today is ZERO. The reporting agent itself states 'this is not a live bug'. The failure it describes requires a FUTURE edit to a different, unchanged file. Calibrated to Suggestion; recorded here rather than fixed, because a pin on resolve-command-trigger.sh's output shape is a contract this PR does not touch. Surfaced in the final report as an advisory follow-up.

fix_decisions.claim_text: PR_NUM derived via grep|head, non-preflight PATH tools deciding a selection
fix_decisions.evidence: CLAUDE.md forbids a non-preflight PATH tool deriving a selection *when a missing tool would silently change the answer*. A missing grep/head empties PR_NUM -> empty HEAD_SHA -> devflow.yml's `[ -z "$HEAD_SHA" ]` branch emits a ::warning:: and renders `CI status unavailable`, and summarize-ci-checks.sh re-checks the same precondition. Fails CLOSED with a specific breadcrumb -- the carve-out the rule permits. The reporting agent graded it LOW and recommended no change.

verification_evidence.ruff: SKIPPED - not installed on this host (exit 127, tool absence, not a permission denial). `python3 -m py_compile lib/test/extract-command-heads.py` passes; CI's `lib + python tests` job runs ruff.
mutation_checks: derived prose pin: dropping Bash(gh pr review:*) from a TOOLS-line copy reports `gh pr review`
derived prose pin: appending `gh pr edit --add-label foo` to a SKILL.md copy's prose reports `gh pr edit` (this is the assertion the completeness critic said did not exist)
duplicate TOOLS line: selector aborts rc=1 naming the count; the fixture's first line grants the head, so without the guard the check would pass vacuously
_jobs_err: a stubbed gh failing the /jobs query leaves 0 temp files behind

reflections: early shadow trigger fired (engine_self_modifying, iteration 1 routed to fixes) and DISAGREED: full-coverage 5/5 roster, 5 new findings, 0 overlap with iteration 1. Decide outcome 2 -> promoted iteration 2.

over-grade calibration gate: S-4 flagged under shape 3 and calibrated Important -> Suggestion with recorded evidence (observable blast radius today is zero; the failure needs a future edit to an unchanged file). S-1 not flagged (source is the completeness critic, not an empirical over-grader).

widens-surface guard changed a decision: VC-24 had been pushed back as claim-quality, but extract-command-heads.py is new code in this PR, so the skip was disqualified and the FAIL fixed rather than deferred. Without that guard this run would have exited with an unresolved checklist FAIL laundered into an APPROVE WITH CAVEAT.

the derived prose scanner passed VACUOUSLY on first write: a stray `cat <<'EOF'` prose span made the heredoc-aware extractor swallow the fixture, so `ungranted` returned empty over an empty head set. Caught only by demanding the fixture surface `gh pr review`. The anti-vacuity assertions exist because of that.
fix-delta gate ran twice (both blinded). Run 1 found a fail-open in a guard iteration 1 had just added (whole-file grep vs the allowlist-scoped matcher); run 2 was clean.

post-shadow edit gate: HEAD advanced past the shadow's reviewed_sha (93ff33c) with the iteration-2 fixes, so a bounded blinded delta-review of 93ff33c..HEAD was run. The verdict does NOT claim shadow agreement over that delta.
user directive mid-run: no further iterations. Iteration 2 is final; remaining advisory items (S-4, F-9) are reported, not fixed.
